visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa for applicants who are intended to travel to italy for an authorized mission from the egyptian and italain authorites.
Once received by the VAC, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy, the next working day & the applicant may be directly contacted by the Embassy of Italy, for any further inquiries and/or a personal interview.

## Visa Fees

[ita > egy > en > Table:Mission > Visa Fees 1]
VFS Global Service Charge

[ita > egy > en > Table:Mission > Visa Fees 2]
- The applicable visa fee has to be paid cash in Egyptian pounds as per the above current exchange rate of euro. It is subject to change without notice
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 6 years of age in the short term category.
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Visa application forms duly filled out and signed by applicant
- Two recent photographs in color, of head on, with the uncovered head for gentlemen and white background
- Passport valid up to 3 months after the expiry of the visa and at least 2 blank pages.
- Previous passports covering the last 7 years or, if none, certificate of movements issued by the Mogamma.
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.
- Copies of any previous and current Schengen, USA and UK visas (if any).
- Verbal note of the corresponding Ministry or employer Organism stating the mission program and the authority covering the expenses related to the mission
- P-to-date personal bank account statement for the last 6 months. In the absence of bank account, proof of other assets has to be provided.
- Justification of the socio professional situation of the traveller.
- Return air Ticket or Reservation indicating the complete itinerary of the tour.
- Travel assistance policy of a duration equal to that requested for visa, valid for all the countries of the Schengen space, that covers the medical expenses and of repatriation in
                                                                            case of accident or disease (minimum cover: € 30.000).
- Common List of Travel Insurance Companies enabled by the Schengen Members in Egypt
click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2KR8PhXyAhsu0f9MsOrnDG/1eaa21089d141b080986d9f07dc3ba27/mission-documents.pdf)
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have white background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should
                                                                            be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses
                                                                            to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a mission stay visa at the Embassy of Italy in Cairo, varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the
                                                                        application is given. Currently, visa processing time is around 15 calendar days in our office of Cairo, and around 20 calendar days in our office of Alexandria for all third country nationals.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Download Forms

## For ease of understanding and submission of document please refer to the checklist attached (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ueL1xnGXpvpO5oX0MrvSC/f3f56fdba08291bf8b1e3052ed0d602a/Mission_checklist.pdf)

## Application Form Ita Arab (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5w8voYI5HGt1ZAd6UACsjq/76d309837b23d997f05dd5255a780dae/mission-Application-Form-Ita-Arab.pdf)

## Application Form Ita Eng (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5vCnRQDcbLC84ejinIbI43/8d1e485541ac361ae28d5593fe54b995/mission-Application-Form-Ita-eng.pdf)

## Download Instructions:

- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer

OR 

- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer

- For more information about the documents required for the study visa for the enrolment in the academic year 2020/2021,
click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3079eDZkafq47aLMrGHhzg/342305fa208fe6616a1e652b49117601/studyvisa.pdf)

- Passport valid up to 3 months after the expiry of the visa and at least 2 blank pages.
- Previous passports covering the last 7 years or, if none, certificate of movements issued by the Mogamma.
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.
- Copies of any previous and current visa (if applicable).
- “Dichiarazione di Valore” (Declaration of Value) of the applicant’s educational certificates duly legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo. The Italian Institution
                                                                            or School should advise the Embassy directly in case it is not required.
- Education Certificates duly legalized by the Consular Office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- Acceptance of enrolment by the Italian School or Institution, stating the duration of course
Documentation  regarding the applicant’s economic status: 
- University Courses or Post graduate  courses:  Proof of means of support in Italy of not less than 350.57 Euro for each month of the academic year. This availability can be proven through Bank Statement of applicant or family.
- Scholarship from Italian Government:
Proof of  scholarship in original.
Where the amount of the scholarship does not satisfy the minimum established by law the availability can be proven throughBank Statement of applicant or family

- Non University  courses :  a) Adequate financial guarantees covering the expenses for the duration of stay 
- b) adequate means for repatriation OR return flight ticket or booking (for courses for period up to 90 days)
- Travel assistance policy of a duration equal to that requested for visa, valid for all the countries of the Schengen space, that covers the medical expenses and of repatriation in case of accident or disease (minimum cover: € 30.000).
- Common List of Travel Insurance Companies enabled by the Schengen Members in Egypt click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2U72d1z7TZoJvjxt6ujwMT/fc59a46f9262697fca6c3ccfa6bd5a70/long-study-documents.pdf)
(For visa  requested more than 90  days) The applicant has to submit his/her original (not scanned) admission letter stapled with the passport. 
- At the time of collection of the passport applicant has to make sure that the admission letter or the declaration letter by the university is duly stamped by the Embassy
- Adequate proof of Boarding and Lodging (if required)
- A return flight ticket or booking (for courses up to 90 days)
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

visa_types.name: Re Entry Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid up to 3 months after the expiry of the visa and at least 1 blank page + Certificate of movements issued by the Mogamma.
- Covering letter from applicant detailing reasons for the re-entry visa application and specifying where he/she resides in Italy
- A - If the “Permesso di soggiorno” (Permit of stay) expiry date does not exceeds 90 days, documentation for not having applied for the renewal in Italy is to be submitted
- B- If application for renewal was submitted but, for personal reasons, applicant had to leave Italy for a long period before its issuance,
- Original of the receipt (given to him by the competent Questura - Police Headquarters or by Poste Italiane) and
- Certificates proving the personal reasons that forced to long stay in Egypt have to be submitted.
- C - If the valid “Permesso di soggiorno” has been stolen or lost while in Egypt, Original Police Report, attesting lost/theft of “Permesso di soggiorno” (Permit of stay), is to be submitted in original, translated in Italian/English & duly attested
- Photocopy of all observations in the passport
- Please note: Re-entry visa can be issued ONLY after authorization from the competent “Questura” (Police Headquarters) in Italy
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy in Cairo the next working day; afterwards, the applicant might be contacted directly by
                                                                        the Embassy for a personal Interview ( if applicable) or for any further inquiries of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the
                                                                        decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents will be available for collection in the Visa Application Centre.

## Visa Fees

[ita > egy > en > Table:Re Entry Visa > Visa Fees 1]
VFS Global Service Charge

[ita > egy > en > Table:Re Entry Visa > Visa Fees 2]
- The applicable visa fee has to be paid cash in Egyptian pounds as per the above current exchange rate of euro. It is subject to change without notice
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 6 years of age in the short term category.
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid up to 3 months after the expiry of the visa and at least 1 blank page + Certificate of movements issued by the Mogamma.
- Covering letter from applicant detailing reasons for the re-entry visa application and specifying where he/she resides in Italy
- A - If the “Permesso di soggiorno” (Permit of stay) expiry date does not exceeds 90 days, documentation for not having applied for the renewal in Italy is to be submitted
- B- If application for renewal was submitted but, for personal reasons, applicant had to leave Italy for a long period before its issuance,
- Original of the receipt (given to him by the competent Questura - Police Headquarters or by Poste Italiane) and
- Certificates proving the personal reasons that forced to long stay in Egypt have to be submitted.
- C - If the valid “Permesso di soggiorno” has been stolen or lost while in Egypt, Original Police Report, attesting lost/theft of “Permesso di soggiorno” (Permit of stay), is to be submitted in original, translated in Italian/English & duly attested
- Photocopy of all observations in the passport
- Please note: Re-entry visa can be issued ONLY after authorization from the competent “Questura” (Police Headquarters) in Italy
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have white background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should
                                                                            be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses
                                                                            to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would not be accepted.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a re-entry visa at the Embassy of Italy in Cairo, varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application
                                                                        is given. Currently, visa processing time is around 15 calendar days in our office of Cairo, and around 20 calendar days in our office of Alexandria for all third country nationals.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

- If both parents are resident in Italy, no objection certification of the local guardian is required.

- Death certificate of the spouse (if applicable).
- If other sons/daughters are residing out of Egypt, prove of their residence abroad.
- Proof of money remittance sent by the son/daughter residing in Italy to his/her parent/s during the last 24 months or sworn Affidavit stating that the father/mother is financially dependent from the son/daughter residing in Italy.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy in Cairo the next working day; afterwards, the applicant might be contacted directly by
                                                                        the Embassy for a personal Interview ( if applicable) or for any further inquiries. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection in the Visa Application Centre.

## Visa Fees

[ita > egy > en > Table:Family Reunion > Visa Fees 1]
VFS Global Service Charge

[ita > egy > en > Table:Family Reunion > Visa Fees 2]
- The applicable visa fee has to be paid cash in Egyptian pounds as per the above current exchange rate of euro. It is subject to change without notice
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 6 years of age in the short term category.
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
FOR SPOUSE: -
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid for at least 18 months from the date of visa application
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.
- Photocopy of: a) the identity card; b) the Residence Permit ; c) Certificate of family composition ("Stato di famiglia") issued by the Italian Municipality, relevant to the personwho has applied for the family reunion authorization in Italy.
- Birth Certificate - duly translated into Italian and then legalized by the Consular office of this Embassy of Italy
- Certificate of family composition ("Stato di famiglia") issued by the competent Egyptian Authority duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- Marriage Certificate duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- If the husband/wife residing in Italy had a previous divorce, sentence/judgment of divorce duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular Office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- Discrepancy, if any, in the details of the applicant on the Nulla Osta should be corrected to correspond with the passport. If not done by the person who has applied for the family reunion authorization in Italy, the Embassy of Italy will request the correction from the competent “Sportello Unico per l'Immigrazione”. This might take longer time.
- If both parents are resident in Italy, no objection certification of the local guardian is required.
FOR PARENTS: - 
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid for at least 18 months from the date of visa application
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.
- Photocopy of: a) the identity card; b) the Residence Permit of the son/daughter who has applied for the family reunion authorization in Italy.
- Birth Certificate - duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo
- Certificate of family composition ("Stato di famiglia") issued by the competent Egyptian Authority, duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- Death certificate of the spouse (if applicable).
- If other sons/daughters are residing out of Egypt, prove of their residence abroad.
- Proof of money remittance sent by the son/daughter residing in Italy to his/her parent/s during the last 24 months or sworn Affidavit stating that the father/mother is financially dependent from the son/daughter residing in Italy.
- Discrepancy, if any, in the details of the applicant on the Nulla Osta should be corrected to correspond with the passport. If not done by the person who has applied for the family  reunion authorization in Italy, the Embassy of Italy will request the correction from the competent Sportello Unico per l'Immigrazione.
This could take longer time.
FOR CHILDREN: - 
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid for at least 18 months from the date of visa application
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.
- Photocopy of: a) the identity card; b) the Residence Permit ; c) Certificate of family composition ("Stato di famiglia") issued by the Italian Municipality, relevant to the person who has applied for the family reunion authorization in Italy.
- Birth Certificate - duly translated into Italian an then legalized by the Consular office of this Embassy of Italy
- Certificate of family composition ("Stato di famiglia") issued by competent Egyptian Authority, duly translated into Italian and legalized by the Consular office of the Embassy of Italy in Cairo.
- Discrepancy, if any, in the details of the applicant on the ‘Nulla Osta’ should be corrected to correspond with the passport. If not done by the person who has applied for the family reunion authorization in Italy, the Embassy of Italy will request the correction to the competent “Sportello Unico per l'Immigrazione”. This could need longer time.
- If both parents are resident in Italy, no objection certification of the local guardian is required.
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have white background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should
                                                                            be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses
                                                                            to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would not be accepted.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a long term family stay visa at the Embassy of Italy in Cairo, varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in
                                                                        which the application is given. Currently, visa processing time is around 15 calendar days in our office of Cairo, and around 20 calendar days in our office of Alexandria for all third country nationals. 
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

visa_types.name: Medical
visa_types.documents_required:
Requisite documents for medical visa
- Application form, fully completed and signed by the applicant
- Two recent passport-size photographs.
- Passport having validity of more than one year with 1 blank page.
- Declaration of 30% payment of the total treatment cost paid to the hospital
- Declaration of the period of stay of the treatment (start date to end date) from the Hospital and Acceptance of the patient at the Hospital in Italy.
- Letter from the local hospital in EGYPT stating the ailment.
- Original & Copy of Passport.
- Financial statement i.e. Salary statements & other bank deposits in Egypt.
- An undertaking ( Formal Declaration) from the local sponsor in Italy stating that he would be taking care of all Medical expenses authenticated & legalized with all the necessary documents (if applicable ).
- A confirmed return air ticket or reservation.
- Proof of accommodation if applicable
Kindly note that the person accompanying the patient will have to provide documents as per the Tourist visa
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian and legalized.

## Overview
This type visa is for applicants who are intended to travel to Italy for any medical purpose

- Invitation letter from the Italian Sport Organization, which is organizing the sport competition, listing all the names of visa applicants, duration of stay, covering of the costs etc., attested by CONI (Italian National Olympic Committee). if applicable
- Proof of adequate means of subsistence
- Supporting letter from Italian or Egyptian Organization.
- proof of economic means of support
Confirmed hotel booking
or
- supporting letter from the Italian organization in this regard.
- Travel assistance policy of a duration equal to that requested for visa, valid for all the countries of the Schengen space, that covers the medical expenses and of repatriation in case of accident or disease (minimum cover: € 30.000).
- Common List of Travel Insurance Companies enabled by the Schengen Members in Egypt
click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3pH4KIEuJUm0aeOzrqZ2Uo/33303c146f9c5d223f33b227b2473fec/sport-documents.pdf)
- If the participant is minor of 18 years: affidavit from both parents giving consent to travel abroad done by public notary.
According to Schengen regulation all the requested documents issued in Arabic have to be translated into Italian language by any translator and legalized.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have white background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should
                                                                            be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses
                                                                            to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a medical stay visa at the Embassy of Italy in Cairo, varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the
                                                                        application is given. Currently, visa processing time is around 15 calendar days in our office of Cairo, and around 20 calendar days in our office of Alexandria for all third country nationals.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy in Cairo the next working day; afterwards, the applicant might be contacted directly by
                                                                        the Embassy for a personal Interview (if applicable) or for any further inquiries. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection in the Visa Application Centre.

## Visa Fees

[ita > egy > en > Table:Employment > Visa Fees 1]
VFS Global Service Charge

[ita > egy > en > Table:Employment > Visa Fees 2]
- The applicable visa fee has to be paid cash in Egyptian pounds as per the above current exchange rate of euro. It is subject to change without notice
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 6 years of age in the short term category.
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled out and signed by applicant.
- Two recent photographs in color (passport-size and white background) of head on, with the uncovered head for gentleman.
- Passport valid for at least 18 months from the date of visa application
- Copy of the passport page containing holder’s data and those mentioning an extension of validity.

## Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in color.
- It should be without border.
- It should have white background only so that features are distinguishable and in contrast against the background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.
- It should be in sharp focus and clear.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 4 cm X 5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, The main characteristics of the face must be apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should
                                                                            be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph must show facing square on to the camera, not looking over the shoulder or tilted, and showing both edges of the face clearly.
- It should be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on the face and no red eye
- If wearing glasses the photograph must show the eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames – lighter framed glasses
                                                                            to be worn). Make sure the frame does not cover any part of the eye.
- The photograph must show alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned & color photocopies would not be accepted.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of an employment stay visa at the Embassy of Italy in Cairo, varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which
                                                                        the application is given. Currently, visa processing time is around 15 calendar days in our office of Cairo, and around 20 calendar days in our office of Alexandria for all third country nationals.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
